campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and money in the treasury. They also discussed plans to improve how people can get around Campbell using different types of transportation like buses, bikes, and walking paths.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasury report and cash position
Multimodal transportation plan to improve transit, biking, and pedestrian options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After listening to residents, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and fast-track it for approval at the next meeting instead of going through the normal lengthy review process.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on proposed ordinance
Council waived first reading requirement to speed up the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next regular council meeting
Community had opportunity to speak and share concerns during hearing

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting covered standard administrative business and moved forward on a construction project, though the full contract details weren't included in the agenda summary.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from multiple March meetings (special sessions, closed sessions, and regular council meetings)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Routine administrative and governance matters

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 14, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to handle several routine matters including managing council member absences and filling open positions on city boards. Most of the meeting focused on wastewater and water projects, including agreements with engineering firms to oversee construction management and approving additional funding for a water recycling pump station project.
keyTopics: Council member absences and board/commission appointments
Hiring engineering firms (HDR and CDM Smith) to manage wastewater facility construction
Additional funding approved for South Bay Water Recycling Pump Station 5 project
Santa Clara Valley Habitat agreement update

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held two sessions on April 14, 2026. In the first part, they reviewed the city's spending plans for building and improving public infrastructure over the next year. In the second part, they recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week with official proclamations.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review housing protections for renters in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new law that would change how the city's tenant protection program works, though specific details of what's changing weren't provided in the agenda.
keyTopics: Tenant protection rules being updated
Housing and Human Services Commission meeting
Review of previous meeting minutes from March
New ordinance affecting renter rights in Sunnyvale

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a workshop to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that shows where Cupertino's money comes from and where it goes. The meeting also covered new rules requiring people who try to influence city decisions on behalf of clients to register and report their lobbying activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
New lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
City budget and spending visibility
Rules for those influencing city legislative or administrative decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council is holding meetings that residents can attend either in person or online via Zoom. The city is making it easy for people to participate remotely and submit their opinions on agenda items before meetings happen.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or join online via Zoom
Remote participation options for residents who can't attend in person
Written public comment submission available through eComments system
Multiple ways to join: Zoom link or phone dial-in available

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030, new project requests from individual council members, and a proposed change to how the city fills vacant council seats. The meeting also included approval of previous meeting minutes and a preview of upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (how federal housing/community development funds will be spent)
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Proposed change to the Council Vacancy Ordinance (how empty council seats get filled)
City Council meeting schedule and upcoming agenda items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a downtown building at 405 Kipling Street that the owner wants to officially recognize as historically significant. If approved, the building would get permission to add 5,000 square feet of new space as a reward for preserving and upgrading the historic structure to meet modern earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building seeking higher historic status
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic safety improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Environmental review exemption approved for the project
